01_initial_request.user_intent: To review translatomics techniques and highlight the application of ribosome profiling in elucidating translational regulatory mechanisms in cancer
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Quantification of translation efficiency (TE) and genome-wide in vivo gene expression

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ribosome profiling enables reliable quantification of genome-wide in vivo gene expression. [Gap] To capture the translating ribosomes, translocation must be halted immediately. [Decision] To achieve accurate snapshot of translation, chose to use elongation inhibitors (e.g., cycloheximide) or flash freezing to perform cell harvesting on proliferating cells, expecting to obtain stalled ribosome-mRNA complexes.

parameters.purpose: prevent ribosomes from running off the mRNA transcripts

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ribosomes stalled on translating transcripts, preserving the in vivo translation state.

references.citation: Ingolia NT, Ghaemmaghami S, Newman JR, Weissman JS. Science. 2009
references.relevance: Original description of ribosome profiling protocol using inhibitors.

references.citation: Wu CC, Zinshteyn B, Wehner KA, Green R. Mol Cell. 2019
references.relevance: Discussion on inhibitor cocktails and conformational states.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cell lysates contain total RNA, most of which is not protected by ribosomes. [Gap] Non-protected RNA must be removed to isolate ribosome footprints. [Decision] To isolate protected fragments, chose to use Ribonuclease (RNase) to perform digestion on cell lysates, expecting to obtain ribosome-protected RNA fragments (RPFs).

parameters.target: RNA regions not protected by ribosomes

02_agent_trajectory.observation: RNA regions not protected by ribosomes cleaved; ribosome-protected fragments (approx. 30 nucleotides) remain.

references.citation: Ingolia NT, Brar GA, Rouskin S, McGeachy AM, Weissman JS. Nat Protoc. 2012
references.relevance: Detailed protocol for RNase digestion in ribosome profiling.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Digested lysates contain ribosome-RNA complexes mixed with other cellular components. [Gap] RPFs need to be physically separated from free RNA and other complexes. [Decision] To collect RPFs, chose to use Ultracentrifugation (Sucrose gradient/cushion) to perform fractionation on digested lysates, expecting to obtain purified ribosome-protected fragments.

parameters.medium: sucrose gradient solutions or 30% sucrose cushion

references.citation: King HA, Gerber AP. Brief Funct Genomics. 2016
references.relevance: Overview of translatome profiling methods including ultracentrifugation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Isolated RPFs may contain contaminating ribosomal RNAs (rRNAs). [Gap] rRNA contamination can lead to mapping distortion. [Decision] To ensure data quality, chose to use rRNA depletion methods to perform cleanup on collected RPFs, expecting to obtain rRNA-depleted RPF library.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Contaminating rRNAs removed; ribosomes removed to collect RPFs.

references.citation: Ingolia NT, Brar GA, Rouskin S, McGeachy AM, Weissman JS. Nat Protoc. 2012
references.relevance: Standard step in ribosome profiling workflow to remove rRNAs.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Purified RPFs are RNA fragments not yet ready for sequencing. [Gap] Sequencing platforms require specific library structures. [Decision] To prepare for sequencing, chose to use Library generation (ligating adaptors/UMI) to perform conversion on RPFs, expecting to obtain sequencing-ready libraries.

parameters.components: adaptors containing priming sites and a unique molecular identifier (UMI)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Sequencing libraries constructed with typical RPF length inserts.

references.relevance: Description of library construction with UMIs for single-cell Ribo-seq.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Libraries contain the genetic information of RPFs. [Gap] The sequence content needs to be read to map ribosome positions. [Decision] To acquire translatomic data, chose to use Deep sequencing to perform sequencing on RPF libraries, expecting to obtain RPF sequence reads.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ribosome profiles generated with precise codon-level positional information.

references.citation: Ingolia NT, Ghaemmaghami S, Newman JR, Weissman JS. Science. 2009
references.relevance: First application of deep sequencing for ribosome profiling.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Translation efficiency requires normalization against mRNA abundance. [Gap] Ribo-seq data alone reflects ribosome density, not efficiency relative to transcript level. [Decision] To calculate TE, chose to use RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) to perform parallel transcriptome analysis on total mRNA, expecting to obtain mRNA abundance data (FPKM).
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Genome-wide mRNA abundance data acquired for normalization.

references.citation: Ingolia NT, Lareau LF, Weissman JS. Cell. 2011
references.relevance: Combined use of RNA-seq and Ribo-seq for TE calculation.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Both Ribo-seq (RPKM) and RNA-seq (FPKM) data are available. [Gap] Need to quantify the rate of protein synthesis relative to transcript abundance. [Decision] To quantify translation efficiency, chose to use TE calculation formula to perform normalization on Ribo-seq and RNA-seq data, expecting to obtain TE values for each gene.

parameters.definition: ratio of translating mRNAs to total mRNAs of a gene

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Translation Efficiency (TE) values calculated for genome-wide genes.

references.citation: Ingolia NT, Ghaemmaghami S, Newman JR, Weissman JS. Science. 2009
references.relevance: Definition of TE calculation using Ribo-seq and RNA-seq.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw sequencing data may contain biases (e.g., CHX treatment, footprint length variation). [Gap] Need to accurately estimate TE and ribosome densities. [Decision] To mitigate experimental distortions, chose to use computational tools (Scikit-ribo, Riboformer) to perform data analysis on Ribo-seq data, expecting to obtain accurate genome-wide A-site prediction and TE estimation.

parameters.Scikit-ribo: codon-level generalized linear model with ridge penalty
parameters.purpose: accurate prediction of ribosome densities at codon resolution

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Accurate genome-wide A-site prediction and TE estimation; context-dependent changes in translation dynamics uncovered.

references.citation: Fang H, et al. Cell Syst. 2018
references.relevance: Development of Scikit-ribo for accurate TE estimation.
Development of Riboformer for predicting translation dynamics.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Review of oncological research advances using Ribosome Profiling

RPF_length.interpretation: Typical length of ribosome-protected fragments used for alignment.

TE_formula.interpretation: Standard metric for quantifying translation efficiency.

application_scope.interpretation: Ability to evaluate gene expression and translation across the entire genome.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective to review translatomics techniques and highlight ribosome profiling application in cancer was achieved. The paper confirms ribosome profiling is a powerful tool for quantifying translation efficiency and elucidating translational regulatory mechanisms in cancer (e.g., metabolic reprogramming, therapy resistance).
